skill.name: incident-response-incident-response
skill.description: Guides incident response workflows with triage, investigation, mitigation, communication, and postmortem steps.
skill.summary: Coordinate production incident response from detection through prevention.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and incident response terminology, with no executable code, no cryptographic implementation, and no hostile reconnaissance instructions. The skill does ask agents to coordinate high-impact operational tasks, so users should apply normal change-control safeguards.

low_findings.title: Operational Change Guidance Requires Human Oversight
low_findings.description: The skill instructs responders to assess rollbacks, execute emergency deployments, and implement production fixes. This is legitimate incident response guidance, but changes to production systems should remain under human approval and existing access controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines clearly describe production mitigation and deployment actions. The concern is operational risk, not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static External Command Finding Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at line 21 is Markdown formatting around a resource path. No shell execution syntax or command invocation is present in the reviewed skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact line contains inline Markdown backticks around resources/implementation-playbook.md. It does not execute a command or interpolate user input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts correspond to ordinary words or incident response text. No cryptographic functions, algorithms, keys, hashing code, or encryption code are present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain descriptive incident response language. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts are incident response investigation steps such as checking affected services, logs, network latency, and dashboards. These are expected defensive workflows, not unauthorized discovery.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The prompts are framed around defensive incident triage and recovery. I found no evidence of scanning third-party targets or collecting data for exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Production incidents require fast triage, clear ownership, and reliable communication. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured response workflow from detection to postmortem.
actual_capabilities: Classifies incidents by severity and business impact.
Coordinates investigation across observability, debugging, security, and performance roles.
Defines mitigation, rollback, deployment, and validation steps.
Creates stakeholder communication and customer impact workflows.
Structures blameless postmortems and prevention action items.
Lists success criteria for immediate response and long-term improvement.
limitations: Does not directly connect to monitoring, alerting, or deployment systems.
Requires accurate incident details, logs, metrics, and system context from the user.
Cannot replace authorized incident commanders or production change controls.
References specialized subagents that may not exist in every AI tool environment.

use_cases.title: Run Production Outage Triage
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to classify severity, identify affected services, assign response roles, and start mitigation.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Security Incident Review
use_cases.description: Guide responders through breach indicators, access patterns, data exposure checks, and security impact documentation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Postmortem Actions
use_cases.description: Turn incident notes into a blameless timeline, root cause summary, owners, deadlines, and monitoring improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Classify an Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this incident response workflow to classify this incident: [describe alerts, affected service, user impact, and recent changes]. Provide severity, initial roles, and first mitigation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Root Cause
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the investigation phase to this incident: [add logs, metrics, traces, errors, and timeline]. Identify likely root cause, contributing factors, and evidence gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Recovery Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the resolution phase for this incident fix: [describe root cause and proposed fix]. Create a staged rollout, validation checks, rollback triggers, and communication points.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Postmortem Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the postmortem and prevention phases for this resolved incident: [paste timeline and decisions]. Produce a blameless summary, action items, owners, monitoring changes, and follow-up risks.

output_examples.input: A checkout service has 40 percent errors after a deployment.
output: Severity: P1 because checkout errors have direct revenue impact.
Initial actions: assign incident commander, review deployment diff, compare error rate by region, and evaluate rollback.
Communication: publish internal update now and prepare customer support notes if impact continues.

output_examples.input: Database latency increased and several APIs are timing out.
output: Investigation focus: query locks, connection pool saturation, slow queries, and dependent service retries.
Mitigation options: reduce noncritical traffic, raise capacity if available, and disable expensive features.
Validation: confirm latency, error rate, and customer impact return to expected ranges.

output_examples.input: The incident is resolved and the team needs follow-up work.
output: Postmortem sections: timeline, root cause, contributing factors, customer impact, what worked, and improvements.
Action items: add early warning alerts, update runbooks, test rollback steps, and assign owners with dates.

best_practices: Provide current alerts, affected services, timeline, and recent changes before starting triage.
Keep a human incident commander responsible for production approvals and stakeholder decisions.
Record decisions, timestamps, evidence, and owners during the incident.
anti_patterns: Do not let the skill perform production changes without authorized human approval.
Do not skip communication updates while technical investigation continues.
Do not treat postmortem action items as complete without owners and deadlines.

faq.question: What type of incidents does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports outages, performance degradation, security incidents, data integrity issues, infrastructure failures, and third-party disruptions.

faq.question: Can it connect to PagerDuty, Grafana, or Splunk?
faq.answer: No. It describes what to inspect, but users must provide data or use separate approved integrations.

faq.question: Does it make production changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can propose mitigation and deployment steps, but humans must approve and execute changes.

faq.question: Is it useful for security incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes steps for attack indicators, access review, data exposure risk, and audit trail analysis.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide alerts, affected services, user impact, recent deployments, relevant logs, dashboards, and known mitigations.

faq.question: Can it help after the incident is over?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes postmortem structure, monitoring improvements, runbook updates, and prevention planning.
